message:
[medium] mailchimp: reject base_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

MailchimpConfig::from_params stored params["base_url"] as Option<String>
verbatim with no URL-shape validation. base_url_policy was only
consulted in provisioning_readiness (advisory — it does not gate
handle_configure). A configure payload like

    {"api_key": "k-us1", "base_url": "https://us1.api.mailchimp.com/3.0?leak=x"}

would pass from_params and flow into MailchimpClient::new where it is
concatenated via format!("{}{path}", self.base_url) in every request
method (client.rs:202/213/224). `?leak=x` would leak on every
Mailchimp API request with the endpoint path parsed as part of the
query. Userinfo (`https://attacker:pw@us1.api.mailchimp.com/3.0`)
would bake into every request URL and silently override the
Authorization header.

- New reject_base_url_qfu helper called from MailchimpConfig::from_params
  (only when base_url is Some — the None default flows through
  extract_dc / base_url_for_dc and is trusted). Rejects userinfo
  (username or password) and query/fragment components at configure
  time. Host-allowlist advisory in base_url_policy is preserved
  unchanged.
- Five new unit tests: accept clean us1.api.mailchimp.com, reject
  query / fragment / userinfo, reject unparseable URL.

Verification: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cargo check -p
fcp-mailchimp --tests is still blocked by the upstream asupersync
main E0252 regression at src/runtime/waker.rs:128. Mailchimp diff
reviewed manually: additions use std url::Url accessor calls with
FcpError shapes identical to the surrounding validator branches, and
the Option<String> wrapping matches existing effective_base_url
logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
